pages.notes: Index page mapping diary page numbers to places visited. Year markers '1989', '1990', and '1991' appear inline with the list entries. 'Long [?Mynd]' — the second word is likely 'Mynd' (Long Mynd, Shropshire) but handwriting is ambiguous. 'Gerniont' and 'Arcon Barros' could be French place names but are uncertain spellings — possibly 'Germont' and 'Arcon-Barros' or similar. 'Chambones' may be 'Chambons'. 'Tetburgs [?Avening] Park' — the middle word is partially cut/unclear, possibly 'Avening'. Entry 102 page number visible at top right of column.

pages.notes: Continuation of index page from left side. Year marker '1992' appears as a horizontal rule entry. Entry 106 has a word struck through before 'Ellesmere' — appears to read '[strikethrough: Fotchild]' or similar, possibly a correction. Two entries share page number 132 (Lac du Serrang and Salbris). '126 [?Tave]' — partially visible at bottom, possibly cut off; then 126 also lists Font de Gaume suggesting the bottom entry continues to next page. 'Entraques' may be 'Entraygues'. 'Casaniouse' — cf. 'Cassaniouze' on left page (same place, different spelling here). 'St. Miers la Ville' may be 'St. Mihiel-la-Ville' or similar. A note 'on' appears in pencil in right margin.
